description_long: This is a round, rustic loaf of bread with a thick, weathered crust that appears mottled with shades of brown, tan, and off-white. The surface is uneven and rough, with patches of flour residue and some darker, almost charred spots, suggesting a traditional baking method such as wood-fired or stone oven. The top of the loaf features a slightly raised, knobby area, possibly where the dough was pinched or scored before baking. The overall shape is somewhat irregular, with a natural, handmade appearance rather than a perfectly symmetrical form. There are no visible slices or cuts, and the loaf appears whole and unsliced. The bread's exterior looks dense and hearty, typical of artisanal or rustic bread found in urban markets or bakeries.
description: A round, rustic loaf of bread with a thick, mottled crust in shades of brown and tan, featuring a rough, flour-dusted surface and an irregular, handmade shape.
description_view_0: This view shows the top of the loaf with a prominent knobby area and a mix of brown and tan crust, with visible rough patches and flour residue.
description_view_1: The side of the loaf is visible here, highlighting a large, pale patch and more pronounced surface texture, with some darker, charred spots.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the rounded, irregular shape and the mottled coloration, with a smoother area and less visible flour compared to other views.
description_view_3: The top and upper side are shown, with the knobby area most prominent and a mix of brown and tan crust, along with some rough, cracked textures.
attribute_crust_pattern: The crust is thick and rugged, with a mottled pattern of brown, tan, and off-white patches. It features rough, cracked textures and some areas dusted with flour, giving it a rustic, artisanal appearance.
